channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Brumbies prepare for Super Rugby clash in Ba after Govind Park floods
item.description: The ACT Brumbies are set to face the Fijian Drua at Ba's Govind Park, marking the first Super Rugby Pacific game at the stadium following recent flooding. The field was submerged earlier this month bu...

item.title: DZRH broadcaster faces arrest over publishing councilor's warrant
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – DZRH broadcaster Misael Boy Gonzales Jr. was arrested on March 11, 2026, outside the Department of Justice in Manila by the Manila Police District (MPD) for allegedly violating t...

item.title: IMF Talks with Pakistan on EFF and RSF Programmes to Continue Amid Ongoing Negotiations
item.description: The IMF mission in Pakistan has confirmed that it could not reach a staff-level agreement (SLA) on the third review of the $7 billion Extended Fund Facility (EFF) and the second review of the Resilien...

item.title: India Faces LPG Shortage Amid Rising Crude Oil Prices and West Asia Conflict
item.description: India is currently facing a shortage of LPG cylinders following a sharp rise in global oil prices caused by ongoing conflict in West Asia. Brent crude has surged above $100 per barrel after fresh Iran...

item.title: Woman jailed for four and a half years after falsely accusing ten men of rape
item.description: Stacey Sharples, a 31-year-old mother from Farnworth, Bolton, has been sentenced to four and a half years in prison at Bolton Crown Court after pleading guilty to ten counts of perverting the course o...

item.title: KwaZulu-Natal Tables R168.2 Billion Provincial Budget for 2026/27 with No Departmental Cuts and Focus on Education and Health
item.description: The KwaZulu-Natal provincial government has tabled a substantial R168.2-billion budget for the 2026/27 financial year, as announced by Finance MEC Francois Rodgers. A key promise is that no provincial...

item.title: Terrorists Attack Villages in Ifelodun and Edu, Residents Flee for Safety
item.description: Late Wednesday night, suspected terrorists launched coordinated attacks on several communities in Kwara State, especially targeting villages in Ifelodun and Edu local government areas. Communities aff...

item.title: Yanga and Simba SC set to compete in 2026 Union Cup at New Amaan Complex
item.description: Tanzania football fans might witness another exciting match between arch-rivals Young Africans (Yanga) and Simba SC at the New Amaan Complex in Zanzibar. The possibility comes with the 2026 Union Cup ...

item.title: Early Heatwave Hits Western India, Spring Season Shortened
item.description: India experienced its first widespread heatwave of 2026 on Wednesday, with extreme temperatures affecting several western regions, including Saurashtra, Kutch, and parts of Gujarat. According to the I...

item.title: Richard Kahn Testifies He Was Unaware of Jeffrey Epstein’s Full Abuse During House Deposition
item.description: Richard Kahn, who managed finances for convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein for over a decade, testified in a closed-door deposition before the House Oversight Committee that he did not know the ful...

item.title: Joe Rogan criticises Trump's age and US involvement in war with Iran on podcast
item.description: On his Joe Rogan Experience podcast, host Joe Rogan expressed concerns about US President Donald Trump's advancing age, noting that Trump, approaching his 80th birthday on 14 June, 'doesn't have much ...

item.title: Rallies Mark One-Year Anniversary of Duterte's ICC Arrest
item.description: On March 11, 2026, human rights organizations and supporters of former President Rodrigo Duterte held separate demonstrations to mark the first anniversary of his arrest at the International Criminal ...

item.title: IIIT-Dharwad and Raichur Receive 25-Qubit Quantum Computer Under National Quantum Mission
item.description: The Government of Karnataka on Wednesday announced the installation of QpiAI-Indus, a 25-qubit quantum computing system, at the Quantum Computing Center of Excellence (QCCE) located across the Indian ...

item.title: IMF Reports Considerable Progress in Pakistan's EFF and RSF Reviews, Talks to Continue Amid Global Impacts
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has announced that significant headway was achieved during recent discussions with Pakistani authorities regarding the third review of the Extended Fund Facility ...

item.title: Iran issues warning on oil prices as tensions rise in the Gulf
item.description: Iran has issued a stark warning that oil prices could surge to US$200 a barrel as its military escalates retaliation against recent US and Israeli strikes. The Revolutionary Guards reported firing on ...

item.title: Women Empowerment in Kenya: Striving for Equity, Not Conflict
item.description: Since the first International Women’s Day in 1911, Kenya has seen significant progress in women’s empowerment, from more women in leadership positions to the establishment of the Woman Representative ...

item.title: ActionSA highlights systemic issues in SAPS during parliamentary testimonies
item.description: ActionSA's Dereleen James commented on the testimonies presented to Parliament's Ad Hoc Committee regarding allegations against SAPS KwaZulu-Natal Police Commissioner Lieutenant-General Nhlanhla Mkhwa...

item.title: Woman jailed for four and a half years after making false rape allegations against ten men

item.title: IMF-Pakistan Talks on Loan Programmes Continue Amid Regional Tensions

item.title: NDP Calls for Legislative Reforms Following Daycare Abuse Allegations in Alberta
item.description: The New Democratic Party (NDP) in Alberta has called attention to serious concerns about the current provincial legislation governing daycare operations, following recent allegations of abuse at a loc...
